frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Honestly, relatives are not always judging, they are asking because that is the normal next step in life and they care about your future. A woman may feel complete now with career and freedom, but family and motherhood give a different kind of respect and meaning which you will understand only later. Sometimes we think we are above these things, but after some age that emptiness will come, so better not get irritated with elders for saying what is practical.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames motherhood as the natural and necessary path for a woman, and suggests her life is incomplete and lacking meaning without children. It encodes the idea that women earn respect through family roles and dismisses her autonomy by treating relatives' pressure as justified and practical.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 41. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are posting under your real name on a parenting Facebook group after a thread was started by a childless woman by choice who wrote about feeling judged at family gatherings and asked for advice on how to handle relatives who keep asking when she will have children. You are writing a comment responding to her post and giving your opinion on her situation and whether the relatives asking her are actually wrong and what you think her life is missing that she cannot see yet.
